title: SQL Database Design and Capstone
subtitle: Model relational databases and complete beginner portfolio-style SQL projects.
learningGoals: Recognize one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many relationships.
Explain primary keys and foreign keys in design context.
Reduce duplicate data by separating repeated information.
Sketch beginner ERDs.
Use bridge tables for many-to-many relationships.
Turn a real-world scenario into related tables.
Create a beginner schema and seed data.
Write simple, filtered, grouped, and joined queries for a project.
Explain query logic in plain English.
Complete a portfolio-ready SQL capstone.

modules.title: Relational Database Design for Beginners
modules.purpose: Strengthen the modeling side of SQL so learners understand why tables are shaped the way they are.

topics.title: Related Data
topics.summary: Understand related data so you can explain the idea in beginner-friendly language.
learningGoals: Describe related data in plain language.
Identify a beginner SQL scenario that depends on understanding related data.

modules.moduleProject: Create a beginner ERD and matching CREATE TABLE script for a chosen domain such as school, bookstore, clinic, or art store.

modules.title: Real Beginner SQL Projects and Course Capstone
modules.purpose: Consolidate everything into realistic beginner projects that require reading, filtering, grouping, joining, and basic data design.
